content:
- Read the choose-task instructions, refreshed .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md, and checked the prioritization skill instructions.
- The refreshed active-task summary contains a single candidate: .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-investigate-and-fix-failing-pipeline.md with <passes>false</passes> and <priority>high</priority>; its task file has no <blocked_by> tags, so it is unblocked and must be chosen by the bug-first rule.
- Confirmed the actual repo stack at a high level from the codebase entrypoints: Nix-driven Make targets, Rust workspace crates, and a vendored Go module under cockroachdb_molt; next step is to write the selected task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and quit.

- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt with the selected task path .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-investigate-and-fix-failing-pipeline.md.
- No other active tasks were eligible because the refreshed summary exposed only this unblocked failing bug task.
- If resumed, proceed by executing the selected bug task and validating the full make check/test/lint gates after the fix.

- Read prior progress entries, .ralph/current_task.txt, and the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill files.
- Confirmed the active task path is .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-investigate-and-fix-failing-pipeline.md and that prior context expected execution of this bug task.
- Next: read the bug task file, determine whether it is in planning or execution state, then either create/update a plan and quit or execute it with TDD.

- Read the selected bug task, confirmed it had no prior execution marker, and handled this turn as plan-only.
- Pulled authoritative GitHub Actions evidence with /home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl: latest failing run is 25101383986 (Publish Images, 2026-04-29T09:32:35Z), failing job 73551223578 `nix flake check`, failing command `nix flake check --print-build-logs --show-trace`.
- Downloaded hosted logs and identified the root failing behavior: long-lane runner tests start `molt verify-service` with stale YAML that still uses `verify.source.url` / `verify.destination.url`; Go verify-service now rejects `url` in DatabaseConfig and requires the typed database mapping shape.
- Read the relevant code paths: crates/runner/tests/support/verify_service_harness.rs and cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice/{config.go,resolved_config.go}. The live boundary smell is the Rust harness hand-rendering deprecated stringly YAML instead of mirroring the real typed verify-service config model.
- Created and marked NOW EXECUTE plan at .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-investigate-and-fix-failing-pipeline_plans/2026-04-29-failing-pipeline-investigation-plan.md.
- Next turn: execute that plan with a focused nix-based repro of the failing long-lane check, replace the stale verify-service YAML seam with typed config rendering plus URL decomposition inside the harness, then validate make check/lint/test and the hosted-equivalent flake check.

- Read prior progress, active task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill definitions.
- Confirmed the selected task is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-investigate-and-fix-failing-pipeline.md`; prior context says a `NOW EXECUTE` plan already exists for this bug.
- Next I am reading the task file, execution plan, and relevant verify-service harness/config code so I can reproduce the failure and execute the fix with vertical TDD.

- Read the task and NOW EXECUTE plan, then confirmed the stale boundary in `crates/runner/tests/support/verify_service_harness.rs`: it hand-writes `verify.source.url` / `verify.destination.url` even though Go `verifyservice.DatabaseConfig` no longer accepts `url` and now requires `verify.databases`.
- Tried the focused hosted-like repro `nix build .#checks.x86_64-linux.runner-crate-nextest-long --print-build-logs --show-trace`, but in this workstation it stops earlier with an architecture mismatch (`Required system: x86_64-linux`, `Current system: aarch64-linux`) before reaching the verify-service failure.
- Next I am finding the smallest current-system command that still exercises the same long-lane verify-service path so RED is local and honest before I patch the harness.

- Reproduced the hosted-class failure honestly on this machine with `nix build .#checks.aarch64-linux.runner-crate-nextest-long --print-build-logs --show-trace`: initially the ignored verify-service tests failed exactly like hosted with `field url not found in type verifyservice.DatabaseConfig`.
- Replaced the stringly YAML block in `crates/runner/tests/support/verify_service_harness.rs` with typed serde-serialized config plus one internal Postgres URL parser and one `verify.databases` mapping.
- The next focused long-lane run moved forward: config now loads, one ignored test passes, and the remaining failures are a new runtime contract mismatch on `POST /jobs` returning `400 Bad Request`. Next I am reading the Go verify-service HTTP contract and job payload requirements to finish the seam update cleanly.

- The typed config + job-request seam is now aligned: the long-lane verify-service path gets past config load and no longer dies on deprecated `include_*` fields.
- A subsequent full long-lane run exposed an unrelated sandbox resource issue: Cockroach startup failed with `store /build/...: out of disk space`, which interrupted the lane before all verify tests could complete.
- Next I am selecting the lowest-pressure Nix-backed validation path that still exercises the repaired verify-service seam, then I will run the required `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and finally the hosted-equivalent flake check.

- Found and started fixing a second stale boundary after the config seam: Rust test support was still decoding an obsolete top-level verify job `failure/result` schema even though Go now returns per-database status, tables, rows, errors, and findings under `databases[]`.
- Updated `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_integrity.rs` and `crates/runner/tests/verify_job_result_contract.rs` toward the canonical per-database response contract.
- Next I am running `make check` to validate the contract refactor quickly before spending more time on the long lane, which is still sensitive to sandbox disk pressure.

- `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` are now green after aligning the verify-service config seam, the verify job request seam, and the verify job response seam.
- The remaining blocker is the long-lane / hosted-equivalent path: the last honest `runner-crate-nextest-long` repro moved past the original pipeline bug but then hit heavy sandbox-space pressure while running many ignored long tests concurrently, obscuring the remaining verify-service correctness result until the response-contract refactor landed.
- Next I am reclaiming more safe cache space and retrying the long-lane / flake validation to see whether the fixed verify-service boundary is now truly green under the affected path.

- The long lane now runs through the repaired config and response seams; remaining failures are no longer about deprecated fields.
- The next root cause appears to be filter semantics: audit state shows `observed_schemas` like `^(public)$`, which indicates the Rust harness is still sending regex-style table filters into the new verify-service job API that expects glob-style `default_schema_match` / `default_table_match` values.
- Next I am replacing that stale regex filter seam with glob-style request values and then rerunning `make check` plus the focused long lane again.

- Fixed the remaining hosted-path bug in the Go layer: `verifyservice.filter.go` was generating non-POSIX regex groups `(?:...)` even though runtime filtering compiles with `regexp.CompilePOSIX`; the long lane now passes after emitting POSIX-compatible groups.
- Current status: `make check` green, focused `runner-crate-nextest-long` green with all 19 ignored tests passing.
- Next I am rerunning `make lint` and `make test` after the final Go filter change, then I will run `nix flake check --print-build-logs --show-trace` and, if still green, close the task.

- Final validation is green: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, focused `checks.aarch64-linux.runner-crate-nextest-long`, and `nix flake check --print-build-logs --show-trace` all passed.
- Marked the bug task done with `<passes>true</passes>` and am now running the Ralph task switch plus preparing the required commit/push.
- If interrupted now, resume from `git status`, verify `.ralph/task_switch.sh` effects, then commit all tracked changes with the required `task finished bug-investigate-and-fix-failing-pipeline: ...` prefix and push.
